name: LoanMax Title Loans
description_short: LoanMax Title Loans offers cash loans secured by your vehicle title, with loans up to $10,000. Founded in 1990 in Georgia, they operate multiple Michigan locations.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, GA, making it one of the longer-standing auto title loan companies in the United States. With over 35 years of operation, the company positions itself as a lender helping working Americans access cash through the equity in their vehicles. Its corporate complaint address is listed in Alpharetta, GA, and it operates branches across multiple states including Michigan, Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, and Nevada, each subject to state-specific licensing and regulatory requirements.

LoanMax provides cash loans using a borrower's vehicle title as collateral. The Southgate, Michigan location on 12850 Eureka Road offers loans up to $10,000. The company requires only three things to apply: the vehicle itself, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear (lien-free) vehicle title. Accepted repayment methods include cash, personal check, debit card, and Western Union transfers. Customers can also manage accounts and make payments online through the company's web portal.

What sets LoanMax apart operationally is its multi-decade track record and its state-by-state licensing structure, which means it operates under direct regulatory oversight in each state it serves. In Michigan, it is licensed by the Department of Insurance and Financial Services. The company maintains consistent branch hours — Monday through Friday 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM — and lists several nearby Michigan locations, giving borrowers options within the metro Detroit area if their nearest branch is inconvenient.

Honestly, LoanMax is a high-cost lender by design. The website discloses no APR, interest rates, or fee schedules for Michigan customers (though Delaware and New Mexico customers can access itemized charge schedules). Nevada-specific disclosures explicitly caution that title loans are for short-term needs only and that borrowers with credit difficulties should seek counseling before borrowing. Because the loan is secured by a vehicle title, failure to repay can result in vehicle repossession. LoanMax is best approached as a last-resort emergency cash tool, not a routine borrowing option.
address: 2825 W U.S. Hwy 50 Suite A, Pueblo, CO 81008

company_info.headquarters: 2825 W U.S. Hwy 50 Suite A, Pueblo, CO 81008

pros: Founded in 1990 — over 35 years in operation as a title lender
Loans up to $10,000 available based on vehicle evaluation
Minimal documentation required: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title only
Accepts multiple payment methods: cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Licensed and regulated in Michigan by the Department of Insurance and Financial Services
Saturday branch hours available (9:00 AM – 2:00 PM)
Multiple Michigan locations in the metro Detroit area for in-person service
cons: No APR, interest rate, or fee information disclosed on the Michigan-facing website
Vehicle title is collateral — failure to repay risks vehicle repossession
Must own a vehicle with a clear (lien-free) title, excluding many borrowers
Branch hours are limited (closed evenings and Sundays)
Company's own Nevada disclosure acknowledges this is a short-term product not suitable as a long-term financial solution
best_for: Vehicle owners with a clear title who need fast cash and cannot qualify for traditional credit
Michigan residents near a metro Detroit branch who prefer in-person loan origination
Borrowers facing a one-time short-term emergency and have a clear repayment plan
Consumers who have exhausted bank and credit union options and need same-day access to $1,000–$10,000
diagnosis: LoanMax is appropriate for vehicle owners with a clear title who need a lump-sum emergency cash advance of up to $10,000 and have a concrete short-term repayment plan. The main caveat is that the website discloses no rate or fee information for Michigan borrowers, and the loan is secured by the vehicle — meaning non-repayment can result in repossession. Borrowers should request full cost disclosure before signing.
services: Auto title loans secured by vehicle equity
In-store loan origination at multiple Michigan branches
Multi-state branch network (MI, VA, DE, SC, NM, NV and others)
